name: ship-learn-next
description: Transform learning content (like YouTube transcripts, articles, tutorials) into actionable implementation plans using the Ship-Learn-Next framework. Use when user wants to turn advice, lessons, or educational content into concrete action steps, reps, or a learning quest.
capabilities: Analyzes transcripts, articles, tutorials to extract actionable lessons
Creates structured Ship-Learn-Next cycles with SHIP-LEARN-NEXT phases
Designs progressive reps that build skills through repetition
Frames specific, measurable learning goals with clear success criteria
Generates complete quest plans with reflection questions
Saves plans as Markdown files with consistent naming conventions
author: softaworks
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. The scanner misidentified documentation patterns as security issues. The skill contains only markdown documentation with example templates and conversation guides - no executable code, no cryptographic operations, and no system access patterns. This is a pure prompting skill with zero runtime risk.
valueStatement: Users struggle to apply educational content because they consume without doing. This skill transforms passive learning (videos, articles, tutorials) into active practice through structured Ship-Learn-Next cycles that build real skills through repetition.
limitations: Does not provide content - users must supply learning material
Does not execute or verify user actions
Does not track progress across sessions
Focuses on individual skill building, not team workflows

useCases.targetUser: Self-directed learners who consume content but struggle to apply it
useCases.title: Video Tutorial to Practice Plan
useCases.description: User watched a YouTube tutorial and wants to actually implement the techniques taught. The skill extracts key lessons and creates a practice plan with concrete reps.

useCases.targetUser: Online course students who want to move beyond passive watching
useCases.title: Course Notes to Action Steps
useCases.description: User has course notes or documentation and wants to practice immediately. The skill identifies the minimal starting point and creates a progressive skill-building path.

useCases.targetUser: Professionals seeking to apply business or technical advice
useCases.title: Article to Implementation Guide
useCases.description: User read an article with advice and wants to take action. The skill converts theoretical advice into specific, shippable tasks with measurable outcomes.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Request
promptTemplates.prompt: I watched this video about [topic]. Here's the transcript: [attach file]. Help me create an actionable plan to actually practice these techniques.
promptTemplates.scenario: User has learning content and wants immediate guidance

promptTemplates.title: Defined Goal
promptTemplates.prompt: I have this [article/tutorial/course notes] about [subject]. I want to achieve [specific goal] in [timeframe]. Create a Ship-Learn-Next plan with concrete reps.
promptTemplates.scenario: User has a clear goal and wants structured learning iterations

promptTemplates.title: Minimal Starting Point
promptTemplates.prompt: I have too much content to process. Help me identify the minimum I need to learn to [complete specific task] this week. Create a small, shippable first rep.
promptTemplates.scenario: User feels overwhelmed and needs a small starting point

promptTemplates.title: Reflection and Iteration
promptTemplates.prompt: I just completed Rep 1 of my learning quest: [describe what happened]. Based on what I learned, create a plan for Rep 2 that adds one new element.
promptTemplates.scenario: User completed an iteration and wants to plan the next step

bestPractices: Push for specific, measurable goals rather than vague intentions like 'learn X'
Start with embarrassingly small reps that can be completed in 1-7 days
Always include reflection questions after each shipped rep to capture learning
antiPatterns: Creating study plans instead of ship plans with actual deliverables
Listing all resources to read instead of identifying minimal resources for current rep
Letting users plan forever without committing to a first ship date

faq.question: What is the Ship-Learn-Next framework?
faq.answer: A three-phase learning cycle: SHIP (create something real), LEARN (reflect honestly on results), NEXT (plan the next iteration based on learnings). It prioritizes doing over studying.

faq.question: What content types work best with this skill?
faq.answer: YouTube transcripts, blog articles, course notes, tutorials, and any educational material with actionable advice. The skill extracts principles and converts them to practice tasks.

faq.question: How long should each rep take?
faq.answer: Each rep should be completable in 1-7 days. Rep 1 should be the smallest possible version - embarrassingly small is better than too ambitious.

faq.question: Does this skill track my progress between sessions?
faq.answer: No. This skill creates plans and helps plan iterations, but does not store or track progress. Users return after completing reps to plan the next step.

faq.question: How many reps should I plan?
faq.answer: The skill typically creates 5 reps with increasing challenge. Each rep adds ONE new element. Details beyond Rep 2-3 will evolve based on actual learning.

faq.question: What makes a good Ship-Learn-Next goal?
faq.answer: Good goals are specific and measurable: 'Get 2 responses from 10 cold emails' not 'Learn sales'. They produce real artifacts and have clear completion criteria.
